plan_type_rationale: 14 active consumers across 5 layers; incompatible schemas; requires phased migration with per-batch type-check and regression gate.

phases.title: Migrate low-complexity single-import consumers (GraphNode-only)
phases.goal: Migrate fuzzy-resolver.ts, ai-prompt-generator.ts, and query-executor.ts off analyzer/graph-builder — these each import only GraphNode. Read pipeline/graph-builder.ts to find or define an equivalent, update the three files, verify tsc --noEmit passes.

tasks.description: Read src/pipeline/graph-builder.ts exports fully — identify which exported types can replace GraphNode, GraphEdge, DependencyGraph from the legacy module
Read src/context/fuzzy-resolver.ts, src/integration/ai-prompt-generator.ts, src/query/query-executor.ts — understand how GraphNode is used in each
Update imports in fuzzy-resolver.ts, ai-prompt-generator.ts, query-executor.ts to use pipeline/graph-builder.ts (or a defined shim if GraphNode has no direct equivalent)
npx tsc --noEmit — confirm Phase 1 files compile cleanly, no new type errors
Run npx vitest — confirm no regressions in existing test suite
Commit Phase 1 migrations

acceptance_criteria: fuzzy-resolver.ts, ai-prompt-generator.ts, query-executor.ts have zero imports from analyzer/graph-builder

phases.title: Migrate medium-complexity RAG consumers (type-only DependencyGraph imports)
phases.goal: Migrate answer-generation-service.ts, chunk-converter.ts, graph-reranker.ts, incremental-indexer.ts, semantic-search.ts — these import DependencyGraph type-only. Determine if ExportedGraph is a compatible substitute or if a shim type alias is needed.

tasks.description: Read each of the 5 RAG consumer files — understand how DependencyGraph is used (parameter types, property access patterns)
Determine whether ExportedGraph from pipeline/graph-builder.ts covers the DependencyGraph usage surface in each file, or whether a compatibility shim is required
Update imports in answer-generation-service.ts, chunk-converter.ts, graph-reranker.ts, incremental-indexer.ts, semantic-search.ts
npx tsc --noEmit — confirm Phase 2 files compile cleanly
Run npx vitest src/integration/rag/ — targeted regression check on RAG layer
Commit Phase 2 migrations

acceptance_criteria: 5 RAG consumer files have zero imports from analyzer/graph-builder

phases.title: Migrate high-complexity consumers (multi-type structural usage)
phases.goal: Migrate graph-to-elements.ts, impact-simulator.ts, multi-hop-traversal.ts, graph-exporter.ts, plugin-graph.ts, and indexing-orchestrator.ts — these use DependencyGraph + GraphNode + GraphEdge structurally and require deeper adaptation.

tasks.description: Read each of the 6 high-complexity files — map all usages of DependencyGraph.nodes (Map), GraphNode, GraphEdge to their pipeline equivalents

For each file, determine: (A) full type replacement is viable, (B) a shim/adapter function is needed, or (C) the consumer must keep legacy types and receive an adapter-wrapped ExportedGraph at call boundaries
Implement the migration for each of the 6 files per the (A)/(B)/(C) ruling — update imports, adapt type usage, add shims where needed
npx tsc --noEmit — confirm all files compile cleanly
Run npx vitest — full regression gate
Commit Phase 3 migrations

acceptance_criteria: All 6 high-complexity files have zero imports from analyzer/graph-builder

phases.title: Delete src/analyzer/graph-builder.ts and final verification
phases.goal: Confirm no remaining imports of analyzer/graph-builder across the entire src/ tree, delete the file, run final tsc and vitest gate.

tasks.description: grep -rn 'analyzer/graph-builder' src/ — confirm zero remaining imports
Delete src/analyzer/graph-builder.ts
npx tsc --noEmit — final type-check with legacy file absent
Run npx vitest — final full regression gate
Commit deletion of src/analyzer/graph-builder.ts

hard_stops.action: Halt. Do not proceed to Phase 2 until Phase 1 compilation and tests are clean.
Halt. Do not proceed to Phase 3 until Phase 2 compilation and tests are clean.
Halt. Do not proceed to Phase 4 (deletion) until Phase 3 is fully clean.
